questions.q: Across what body of water did Hanuman leap to reach Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman bounded across the great ocean that separated the mainland from the island of Lanka.

questions.q: Whom was Hanuman searching for when he leaped to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman crossed the sea to find Sita, who was held captive in Lanka by Ravana.

questions.q: Who held Sita captive in the city Hanuman leaped toward?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the demon king of Lanka, had abducted Sita and imprisoned her there.

questions.q: What did Hanuman do to his body before making the great leap?
options: Became as small as a cat
questions.explanation: Hanuman expanded himself to a colossal size to gather the strength for the mighty leap.

questions.q: From which mountain did Hanuman launch his leap across the sea?
questions.explanation: Hanuman gathered himself on Mount Mahendra and sprang from its summit toward Lanka.

questions.q: Hanuman is the son of which deity?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the son of Vayu, the wind god, which gave him the power to fly through the air.

questions.q: On which island kingdom was Sita imprisoned?
questions.explanation: Sita was held in Lanka, the island fortress ruled by Ravana.

questions.q: What kind of creatures did Hanuman encounter as trials during his flight over the sea?
questions.explanation: Hanuman overcame trials posed by sea-demons during his crossing of the ocean.

questions.q: Which king did Hanuman serve, in whose cause he made the leap?
questions.explanation: Hanuman acted as the devoted servant of Rama, leaping the sea to find Rama's wife Sita.

questions.q: To what race of beings did Hanuman belong?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was a vanara, one of the monkey heroes who aided Rama in the search for Sita.

questions.q: How long was Hanuman's leap across the ocean said to be?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana describes Hanuman bounding roughly a hundred yojanas across the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: The book of the Ramayana that narrates Hanuman's leap and search in Lanka is named after whom?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's journey is told in the Sundara Kanda, the 'Beautiful Book' of the Valmiki Ramayana.

questions.q: Which golden mountain rose from the sea and offered Hanuman a place to rest mid-flight?
questions.explanation: The mountain Mainaka rose from the ocean to offer Hanuman rest, sent by the sea out of gratitude to Hanuman's father, the wind.

questions.q: Why did Hanuman decline the mountain Mainaka's offer to rest?
options: He feared the mountain was a demon's trick
He had vowed not to pause until his task was done
He could not see the mountain clearly
He was already too tired to stop
questions.explanation: Hanuman touched the mountain in courtesy but refused to halt, determined to finish his mission without resting.

questions.q: Surasa, the mother of serpents (nagas), tested Hanuman during his flight. How did he pass her trial?
options: He shrank tiny, entered her mouth, and instantly came out
He put her to sleep with a hymn
questions.explanation: Hanuman shrank to a thumb's size, darted into and out of Surasa's mouth, satisfying her boon and proving his wit.

questions.q: Surasa's test of Hanuman was meant to measure what quality?
questions.explanation: The gods sent Surasa to test Hanuman's strength and intelligence, both of which he displayed by his clever escape.

questions.q: A demoness who seized the shadows of flying creatures tried to drag Hanuman down by his shadow. What was her name?
questions.explanation: Simhika, a sea-demoness, caught Hanuman by his shadow, but he slew her by diving into her body and tearing her apart.

questions.q: How did Hanuman defeat the shadow-seizing demoness Simhika?
options: He grew huge, then shrank and tore her from within
questions.explanation: Hanuman expanded his body, then suddenly shrank and entered Simhika, ripping her apart from inside and emerging.

questions.q: Before Hanuman leaped, what did the assembled vanaras realize about their group's ability to cross the sea?
options: Only Hanuman among them could leap the full distance
All of them could cross easily
None could cross, so they turned back
They would build a bridge instead
questions.explanation: When each vanara measured his leaping power, it became clear that only Hanuman possessed the strength to span the hundred-yojana sea.

questions.q: Who reminded Hanuman of his own immense, half-forgotten powers, rousing him to attempt the leap?
questions.explanation: The wise old bear Jambavan recalled to Hanuman the greatness of his hidden strength, inspiring him to undertake the leap.

questions.q: What happened to the trees and rocks of Mount Mahendra at the moment Hanuman sprang?
options: They were torn loose and flung into the air by the force
questions.explanation: The tremendous force of Hanuman's launch uprooted trees and tore loose rocks, hurling them upward with him.

questions.q: After clearing the ocean, on which mountain near Lanka did Hanuman first alight?
questions.explanation: Hanuman landed on the heights overlooking Lanka, the Trikuta range on which Ravana's city was built.

questions.q: The mountain Mainaka had once possessed wings and fled into the sea to escape whom?
questions.explanation: Mainaka had hidden in the ocean to escape Indra, who clipped the wings of mountains with his thunderbolt; it sheltered there by the sea's favor.

questions.q: Why did the ocean (Sagara) urge Mount Mainaka to rise and aid Hanuman?
options: Out of gratitude to Hanuman's father, the wind god Vayu
questions.explanation: The ocean asked Mainaka to offer rest as a return of past kindness shown by the wind god, Hanuman's father, who had once protected the mountain.

questions.q: Surasa appeared in Hanuman's path because she was sent by which group?
questions.explanation: In the Valmiki Ramayana the gods sent Surasa, mother of the nagas, to assess Hanuman's prowess before he reached Lanka.

questions.q: When Surasa expanded her mouth ever wider to swallow Hanuman, how did he respond?
options: He grew larger to match her, then suddenly shrank thumb-sized
He flew straight down her throat and stayed
He summoned his father Vayu to blow her away
questions.explanation: Hanuman kept growing to match Surasa's widening jaws, then abruptly shrank to a thumb's size, slipping in and out before she could close her mouth.

questions.q: What unusual ability did the demoness Simhika possess that made her dangerous to flying beings?
options: She could seize and hold a creature's shadow on the water
questions.explanation: Simhika, the chaya-graha, could grasp the shadow that a flying creature cast upon the sea and so drag it down to her.

questions.q: A 'yojana,' the unit used to measure Hanuman's leap, is best described as a measure of what?
questions.explanation: A yojana is an ancient Indian unit of distance, so the 'hundred-yojana' leap describes the breadth of the sea Hanuman crossed.

questions.q: Whose detailed report of Sita's location across the sea had earlier set the stage for the leap?
questions.explanation: The wingless vulture Sampati told the searching vanaras that Sita was held in Lanka across the ocean, prompting the leap.

questions.q: As Hanuman approached Lanka after the leap, what time of day did he choose to enter the city for stealth?
options: After nightfall, in a shrunken form
questions.explanation: Hanuman waited and entered Lanka by night, shrinking to a small size so he could slip past Ravana's guards unseen.
